stories.story: China's state prosecutors on Friday formally charged Zhou Yongkang, 72, the country's former security czar, with accepting bribes, abuse of power, and leaking state secrets. This makes Zhou Yongkang, 72, the highest-ranking Chinese Communist Party official ever to face corruption charges. The Supreme People's Procuratorate, the highest prosecution authority in China, stated that prosecutors accused Zhou Yongkang, 72, of taking advantage of his posts to seek benefits for others and illegally accepting huge amounts of money. His alleged actions caused heavy losses to public assets and greatly harmed national interests. Zhou Yongkang, 72, was notified of his legal rights during the investigation, and his lawyer's views were heard.
key_facts: Zhou Yongkang, 72, formally charged on Friday
Zhou Yongkang, 72, charged with accepting bribes, abuse of power, and leaking state secrets
Zhou Yongkang, 72, is the highest-ranking Chinese Communist Party official ever to face corruption charges
Supreme People's Procuratorate announced the charges
Zhou Yongkang, 72, accused of illegally accepting huge amounts of money
Zhou Yongkang's alleged actions caused heavy losses to public assets and harmed national interests
Zhou Yongkang, 72, was notified of legal rights and his lawyer's views were heard
explicit_unknowns: The specific date of 'Friday' is not stated
The exact amount of money Zhou Yongkang, 72, illegally accepted is not stated
The specific public assets and national interests harmed are not stated

stories.thread: Zhou Yongkang's Power and Downfall Timeline
stories.story: Zhou Yongkang, 72, a member of the ruling Communist Party's Politburo Standing Committee and China's top decision-making body, was one of nine men who effectively ruled the country of more than 1.3 billion people. At the height of his power, Zhou Yongkang, 72, controlled police forces, spy agencies, court systems, and prosecution offices across China, deploying vast assets to crush dissent. Zhou Yongkang, 72, retired in 2012 and has not been seen in public since he attended an anniversary event at his alma mater in October 2013. He was expelled from the Communist Party and arrested last December. State media painted an intricate web of officials, cronies, and tycoons orbiting around Zhou Yongkang, 72, before the crumbling of his power structure last summer.
key_facts: Zhou Yongkang, 72, was a member of the Politburo Standing Committee and China's top decision-making body
Zhou Yongkang, 72, was one of nine men who effectively ruled China
Zhou Yongkang, 72, controlled police forces, spy agencies, court systems, and prosecution offices

Zhou Yongkang, 72, last seen in public in October 2013
Zhou Yongkang, 72, was expelled from the Communist Party and arrested last December
State media reported a web of officials, cronies, and tycoons around Zhou Yongkang, 72, before his power structure crumbled last summer

The specific date of 'last summer' is not stated
The name of Zhou Yongkang's alma mater is not stated

stories.thread: Zhou Yongkang's Personal Misconduct and Trial Details
stories.story: Zhou Yongkang, 72, China's former security czar, and his family members were said to have accumulated enormous wealth in a blatant exchange between money and power. State-run Xinhua news agency reported last year that Zhou Yongkang, 72, was also found to have affairs with multiple women and allegedly traded power for sex. His case will be tried in Tianjin, a city near the Chinese capital, Beijing. The president of China's supreme court recently told reporters there would be "open" trials for accused former leaders like Zhou Yongkang, 72. However, the charge of leaking state secrets may allow authorities to shield certain legal proceedings from public view in Zhou Yongkang's case.
key_facts: Zhou Yongkang, 72, and his family members accumulated enormous wealth
Zhou Yongkang, 72, allegedly traded money for power
Zhou Yongkang, 72, had affairs with multiple women and allegedly traded power for sex

The charge of leaking state secrets may shield Zhou Yongkang's legal proceedings from public view

stories.thread: President Xi Jinping's Anti-Corruption Campaign and Zhou Yongkang's Significance
stories.story: President Xi Jinping has been spearheading a massive anti-corruption campaign in China, targeting both "tigers" (high-ranking officials) and "flies" (low-level officials). Zhou Yongkang, 72, China's former security czar, is by far the biggest "tiger" caught in President Xi Jinping's dragnet to date. Longtime political analyst Willy Lam, a professor at the Chinese University of Hong Kong, stated last year that President Xi Jinping has proven powerful enough to break the taboo of never incriminating former Politburo Standing Committee members. Analysts have viewed Zhou Yongkang's shocking downfall as a watershed moment in the secretive world of Chinese politics, now ruled by President Xi Jinping. Willy Lam has predicted a suspended death sentence for Zhou Yongkang, 72.
key_facts: President Xi Jinping is spearheading a massive anti-corruption campaign in China
The campaign targets 'tigers' (high-ranking officials) and 'flies' (low-level officials)
Zhou Yongkang, 72, is the biggest 'tiger' caught in President Xi Jinping's campaign
Willy Lam, a professor at the Chinese University of Hong Kong, noted President Xi Jinping's power
President Xi Jinping broke the taboo of not incriminating former Politburo Standing Committee members
Zhou Yongkang's downfall is viewed as a watershed moment in Chinese politics, now ruled by President Xi Jinping
Willy Lam predicted a suspended death sentence for Zhou Yongkang, 72

The specific details of the 'watershed moment' are not stated

stories.thread: Zhou Yongkang's Connection to Bo Xilai and Factional Struggles
stories.story: Many observers note Zhou Yongkang's patronage of Bo Xilai, a former Communist leader sentenced to life in prison for corruption in 2013. Bo Xilai, the former Chongqing Communist Party chief, was once considered President Xi Jinping's main challenger for the top spot of Chinese leadership. Bo Xilai's spectacular downfall the year before, complete with tales of murder, bribery, and betrayal, attracted global attention. State media cited Bo Xilai's subsequent conviction as a prime example of President Xi Jinping's resolve to clean up the party. Political watchers see similarities between the Bo Xilai and Zhou Yongkang, 72, cases, with analyst Willy Lam stating that those investigated for corruption are on the losing side of factional struggles.
key_facts: Zhou Yongkang, 72, patronized Bo Xilai
Bo Xilai, a former Communist leader, was sentenced to life in prison for corruption in 2013
Bo Xilai was the former Chongqing Communist Party chief
